transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Shelby. How are you doing today?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi. I'm Megan. Honestly not great. I've been thinking about calling for a while but kept talking myself out of it.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm really glad you didn't talk yourself out of it today, Megan. What's going on?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I've been drinking pretty heavily for about three years. I know I need to stop. I just keep finding reasons not to deal with it.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I hear that. Sometimes the hardest part is making that first call, and you've already done it. What kind of reasons have been coming up for you?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Money, mostly. I don't know if I can afford this. And honestly I'm not sure I'm ready. What if I go and it doesn't work?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Those are really honest things to say and I want to take them seriously. Let's start with the readiness piece, because I think it matters most. The fact that you've been thinking about this for a while and you still picked up the phone — that actually tells me something. Can I ask: what made today the day you finally called?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: My daughter said something to me last night. She's eight. She said she was scared of me when I drink. I didn't sleep after that.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for sharing that. That's a profound moment — and you're taking it seriously, which is what matters. Megan, 'ready' doesn't always mean feeling confident. Sometimes it means being willing to try even when you're scared. And you're clearly willing. Now let's talk about cost, because I don't want that to be the thing that stops you.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I have insurance but I don't know what it covers.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's the right first step. Do you have your insurance card? I can note your information and have our benefits team give you an exact answer within two hours — before you commit to anything.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Sure. I have Meridian Care. Member ID BP22149395, group GRP-588547.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. I've noted that. Now — most plans cover substance use treatment at a significant level, often 80 to 100 percent once your deductible is met. But if there is a gap, we also have a sliding-scale fee and financing through CareCredit. The point is: we have never turned someone away because of cost alone. That's not who we are.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Okay. That helps to hear.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: And the 'what if it doesn't work' question — I want to answer that honestly. No one can promise you it will. What I can promise is that not trying guarantees the outcome you're trying to avoid. Treatment gives you a real chance, and we'll work with you to find the right level of care — whether that's outpatient so you can be home with your daughter, or something more intensive.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: The outpatient piece matters. I can't be away from her.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Then we build around that. Our IOP program is three evenings a week. You'd be home every night. Let me have our admissions coordinator call you this afternoon with your benefits information and walk you through that schedule. Can you take a call between two and four?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. I can do that.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: You'll hear from us by four o'clock. And Megan — what you said about your daughter? Hold onto that. That's your reason.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thank you. I needed to hear all of this.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Exemplary objection handling: acknowledged readiness objection before responding, asked a powerful open question ('what made today the day'), addressed cost concretely without dismissing it, gave an honest answer to the 'what if it doesn't work' question. Clinical screen was lighter (scored 3) — agent focused on motivational work, which was appropriate, but severity and withdrawal risk could have been explored.
